records.ety: [F. mollifier, L. mollificare; mollis soft + -ficare (in comp.) to make. See Enmollient, Moil, v. t., and -fy.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Mollified (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Mollifying (?).]

defs: To soften; to make tender; to reduce the hardness, harshness, or asperity of; to qualify; as, to mollify the ground.
quotes: With sweet science mollified their stubborn hearts.

defs: To assuage, as pain or irritation, to appease, as excited feeling or passion; to pacify; to calm.
